description: Fix deploy.sh PROJECT_DIR hardcoded to /root/koris-next which conflicts with INSTALL_DIR=/opt/koris-next used by the installers and koris.sh management CLI. The koris.sh update command does cd /opt/koris-next and then calls deploy.sh, but deploy.sh ignores this and cd's to /root/koris-next. Also add input validation to install.sh for DB_NAME and DB_USER prompts to prevent SQL injection.
steps: In deploy.sh: Replace the hardcoded PROJECT_DIR='/root/koris-next' with dynamic detection: PROJECT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)" which resolves to whichever directory the script is running from (the git checkout). This way it works from both /root/koris-next (legacy) and /opt/koris-next (installer). Also update INSTALL_DIR to use the same pattern or keep it as the fixed /opt/koris-next deployment target.

In install.sh: Add input validation for DB_NAME and DB_USER after the prompts. Both should match ^[a-zA-Z0-9_]+$ pattern (MySQL identifier safe characters). Add: [[ ! "$DB_NAME" =~ ^[a-zA-Z0-9_]+$ ]] && fatal 'Invalid DB name (alphanumeric and underscore only)' and same for DB_USER.
In install.sh: Add validation for PANEL_PORT as numeric: [[ ! "$PANEL_PORT" =~ ^[0-9]+$ ]] && fatal 'Port must be numeric'
acceptance_criteria: deploy.sh uses dynamic PROJECT_DIR based on script location instead of hardcoded /root/koris-next
install.sh validates DB_NAME and DB_USER input format before using in SQL
All scripts pass bash -n syntax check
koris.sh update command would correctly invoke deploy.sh from /opt/koris-next
verification: Run: grep -n 'PROJECT_DIR' deploy.sh to confirm dynamic detection
Run: grep -n 'DB_NAME.*=~' install.sh to confirm validation exists
findings: deploy.sh PROJECT_DIR now uses BASH_SOURCE dynamic detection. install.sh validates DB_NAME, DB_USER (alphanumeric+underscore), and PANEL_PORT (numeric) after prompts. All scripts pass bash -n syntax check.
